exampleSentencesTranslated: Don't forget to bring the keys home okay.
Remember to call me when you arrive okay.
This dish is very spicy, just try a little bit okay.
notes_md:
**đâu nha** is a soft, gentle add-on at the end of a sentence. It makes a reminder, warning, or request sound lighter — like “okay?”, “just so you know,” or “don’t forget.”

It usually comes at the **end of a sentence** to soften the tone, especially when reminding someone about something important. *đâu* adds a slight emphasis (often protective or corrective), and *nha* makes it friendly.


It’s similar in feeling to **nhé**, but **đâu nha** can sound a bit more protective or cautioning.
notes:
đâu nha is a soft, gentle add-on at the end of a sentence. It makes a reminder, warning, or request sound lighter — like “okay?”, “just so you know,” or “don’t forget.”

It usually comes at the end of a sentence to soften the tone, especially when reminding someone about something important. đâu adds a slight emphasis (often protective or corrective), and nha makes it friendly.

It’s similar in feeling to nhé, but đâu nha can sound a bit more protective or cautioning.
exampleSentences: Em đừng quên mang chìa khóa về nhà đâu nha.
Chị nhớ gọi cho anh khi đến nơi đâu nha.
Món này cay lắm, anh ăn thử một chút thôi đâu nha.
